<pr_description>
Consider the following PR description:
pydicom should not define handler, formatter and log level.
The `config` module (imported when pydicom is imported) defines a handler and set the log level for the pydicom logger. This should not be the case IMO. It should be the responsibility of the client code of pydicom to configure the logging module to its convenience. Otherwise one end up having multiple logs record as soon as pydicom is imported:

Example:
```
Could not import pillow
2018-03-25 15:27:29,744 :: DEBUG :: pydicom 
  Could not import pillow
Could not import jpeg_ls
2018-03-25 15:27:29,745 :: DEBUG :: pydicom 
  Could not import jpeg_ls
Could not import gdcm
2018-03-25 15:27:29,745 :: DEBUG :: pydicom 
  Could not import gdcm
``` 
Or am I missing something?

</pr_description>

# Copyright 2008-2018 pydicom authors. See LICENSE file for details.
"""Pydicom configuration options."""

# doc strings following items are picked up by sphinx for documentation

import logging

# Set the type used to hold DS values
#    default False; was decimal-based in pydicom 0.9.7
use_DS_decimal = False


data_element_callback = None
"""Set data_element_callback to a function to be called from read_dataset
every time a RawDataElement has been returned, before it is added
to the dataset.
"""

data_element_callback_kwargs = {}
"""Set this to use as keyword arguments passed to the data_element_callback
function"""


def reset_data_element_callback():
    global data_element_callback
    global data_element_callback_kwargs
    data_element_callback = None
    data_element_callback_kwargs = {}


def DS_decimal(use_Decimal_boolean=True):
    """Set DS class to be derived from Decimal (True) or from float (False)
    If this function is never called, the default in pydicom >= 0.9.8
    is for DS to be based on float.
    """
    use_DS_decimal = use_Decimal_boolean
    import pydicom.valuerep
    if use_DS_decimal:
        pydicom.valuerep.DSclass = pydicom.valuerep.DSdecimal
    else:
        pydicom.valuerep.DSclass = pydicom.valuerep.DSfloat


# Configuration flags
allow_DS_float = False
"""Set allow_float to True to allow DSdecimal instances
to be created with floats; otherwise, they must be explicitly
converted to strings, with the user explicity setting the
precision of digits and rounding. Default: False"""

enforce_valid_values = False
"""Raise errors if any value is not allowed by DICOM standard,
e.g. DS strings that are longer than 16 characters;
IS strings outside the allowed range.
"""

datetime_conversion = False
"""Set datetime_conversion to convert DA, DT and TM
data elements to datetime.date, datetime.datetime
and datetime.time respectively. Default: False
"""

# Logging system and debug function to change logging level
logger = logging.getLogger('pydicom')
handler = logging.StreamHandler()
formatter = logging.Formatter("%(message)s")
handler.setFormatter(formatter)
logger.addHandler(handler)


import pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.numpy_handler as np_handler  # noqa
import pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.rle_handler as rle_handler  # noqa
import pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.pillow_handler as pillow_handler  # noqa
import pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.jpeg_ls_handler as jpegls_handler  # noqa
import pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.gdcm_handler as gdcm_handler  # noqa

pixel_data_handlers = [
    np_handler,
    rle_handler,
    gdcm_handler,
    pillow_handler,
    jpegls_handler,
]
"""Handlers for converting (7fe0,0010) Pixel Data.
This is an ordered list that the dataset.convert_pixel_data()
method will try to extract a correctly sized numpy array from the
PixelData element.

Handers shall have two methods:

def supports_transfer_syntax(ds)
  This returns True if the handler might support the transfer syntax
  indicated in the dicom_dataset

def get_pixeldata(ds):
  This shall either throw an exception or return a correctly sized numpy
  array derived from the PixelData.  Reshaping the array to the correct
  dimensions is handled outside the image handler

The first handler that both announces that it supports the transfer syntax
and does not throw an exception, either in getting the data or when the data
is reshaped to the correct dimensions, is the handler that will provide the
data.

If they all fail, the last one to throw an exception gets to see its
exception thrown up.

If no one throws an exception, but they all refuse to support the transfer
syntax, then this fact is announced in a NotImplementedError exception.
"""


def debug(debug_on=True):
    """Turn debugging of DICOM file reading and writing on or off.
    When debugging is on, file location and details about the
    elements read at that location are logged to the 'pydicom'
    logger using python's logging module.

    :param debug_on: True (default) to turn on debugging,
    False to turn off.
    """
    global logger, debugging
    if debug_on:
        logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
        debugging = True
    else:
        logger.setLevel(logging.WARNING)
        debugging = False


# force level=WARNING, in case logging default is set differently (issue 103)
debug(False)

# Copyright 2008-2018 pydicom authors. See LICENSE file for details.
"""pydicom package -- easily handle DICOM files.
   See Quick Start below.

-----------
Quick Start
-----------

1. A simple program to read a dicom file, modify a value, and write to a new
   file::

    from pydicom.filereader import dcmread
    dataset = dcmread("file1.dcm")
    dataset.PatientName = 'anonymous'
    dataset.save_as("file2.dcm")

2. See the files in the examples directory that came with this package for more
   examples, including some interactive sessions.

3. Learn the methods of the Dataset class; that is the one you will work with
   most directly.

4. Questions and comments can be directed to the pydicom google group:
   http://groups.google.com/group/pydicom

5. Bugs and other issues can be reported in the issue tracker:
   https://www.github.com/pydicom/pydicom

"""


from pydicom.dataelem import DataElement
from pydicom.dataset import Dataset, FileDataset
from pydicom.filereader import dcmread, read_file
from pydicom.filewriter import dcmwrite, write_file
from pydicom.sequence import Sequence

from ._version import __version__, __version_info__

__all__ = ['DataElement',
           'Dataset',
           'FileDataset',
           'Sequence',
           'dcmread',
           'dcmwrite',
           'read_file',
           'write_file',
           '__version__',
           '__version_info__']

from pydicom.compat import in_py2
if in_py2:
    import warnings
    msg = 'Python 2 will no longer be supported after the pydicom v1.4 release'
    warnings.warn(msg, DeprecationWarning)

"""Pydicom configuration options."""

# doc strings following items are picked up by sphinx for documentation

import logging

# Set the type used to hold DS values
#    default False; was decimal-based in pydicom 0.9.7
use_DS_decimal = False


data_element_callback = None
"""Set data_element_callback to a function to be called from read_dataset
every time a RawDataElement has been returned, before it is added
to the dataset.
"""

data_element_callback_kwargs = {}
"""Set this to use as keyword arguments passed to the data_element_callback
function"""


def reset_data_element_callback():
    global data_element_callback
    global data_element_callback_kwargs
    data_element_callback = None
    data_element_callback_kwargs = {}


def DS_decimal(use_Decimal_boolean=True):
    """Set DS class to be derived from Decimal (True) or from float (False)
    If this function is never called, the default in pydicom >= 0.9.8
    is for DS to be based on float.
    """
    use_DS_decimal = use_Decimal_boolean
    import pydicom.valuerep
    if use_DS_decimal:
        pydicom.valuerep.DSclass = pydicom.valuerep.DSdecimal
    else:
        pydicom.valuerep.DSclass = pydicom.valuerep.DSfloat


# Configuration flags
allow_DS_float = False
"""Set allow_float to True to allow DSdecimal instances
to be created with floats; otherwise, they must be explicitly
converted to strings, with the user explicity setting the
precision of digits and rounding. Default: False"""

enforce_valid_values = False
"""Raise errors if any value is not allowed by DICOM standard,
e.g. DS strings that are longer than 16 characters;
IS strings outside the allowed range.
"""

datetime_conversion = False
"""Set datetime_conversion to convert DA, DT and TM
data elements to datetime.date, datetime.datetime
and datetime.time respectively. Default: False
"""

# Logging system and debug function to change logging level
logger = logging.getLogger('pydicom')


import pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.numpy_handler as np_handler  # noqa
import pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.rle_handler as rle_handler  # noqa
import pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.pillow_handler as pillow_handler  # noqa
import pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.jpeg_ls_handler as jpegls_handler  # noqa
import pydicom.pixel_data_handlers.gdcm_handler as gdcm_handler  # noqa

pixel_data_handlers = [
    np_handler,
    rle_handler,
    gdcm_handler,
    pillow_handler,
    jpegls_handler,
]
"""Handlers for converting (7fe0,0010) Pixel Data.
This is an ordered list that the dataset.convert_pixel_data()
method will try to extract a correctly sized numpy array from the
PixelData element.

Handers shall have two methods:

def supports_transfer_syntax(ds)
  This returns True if the handler might support the transfer syntax
  indicated in the dicom_dataset

def get_pixeldata(ds):
  This shall either throw an exception or return a correctly sized numpy
  array derived from the PixelData.  Reshaping the array to the correct
  dimensions is handled outside the image handler

The first handler that both announces that it supports the transfer syntax
and does not throw an exception, either in getting the data or when the data
is reshaped to the correct dimensions, is the handler that will provide the
data.

If they all fail, the last one to throw an exception gets to see its
exception thrown up.

If no one throws an exception, but they all refuse to support the transfer
syntax, then this fact is announced in a NotImplementedError exception.
"""


def debug(debug_on=True):
    """Turn debugging of DICOM file reading and writing on or off.
    When debugging is on, file location and details about the
    elements read at that location are logged to the 'pydicom'
    logger using python's logging module.

    :param debug_on: True (default) to turn on debugging,
    False to turn off.
    """
    global logger, debugging
    if debug_on:
        logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
        debugging = True
    else:
        logger.setLevel(logging.WARNING)
        debugging = False


# Initialize debugging flag
debugging = False
EOF"}

```
Could not import pillow
2018-03-25 15:27:29,744 :: DEBUG :: pydicom

```

This looks like duplicate messages - one without formatting and one with formatting. That's exactly the issue we fixed! The unformatted message was from pydicom's handler, and the formatted one was from the user's configuration.

"""
Reproduce the exact issue from the PR description.
Before fix: Would show duplicate log messages when pydicom is imported
After fix: Should only show one formatted message
"""
import logging
import sys

# Configure logging similar to what a user might do
logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.DEBUG,
    format='%(asctime)s :: %(levelname)s :: %(name)s \n  %(message)s',
    datefmt='%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'
)

print("=" * 70)
print("Reproducing PR issue: pydicom should not define handler/formatter/level")
print("=" * 70)
print("\nBefore the fix, importing pydicom would cause duplicate log messages:")
print("1. An unformatted message from pydicom's handler")
print("2. A formatted message from the user's configuration")
print("\nAfter the fix, we should only see formatted messages.")
print("\nNow importing pydicom...")
print("-" * 70)

import pydicom

print("-" * 70)
print("\nImport complete.")
print("\nIf the fix is working correctly:")
print("1. We should NOT see duplicate messages")
print("2. Any log messages should have the format we specified")
print("3. The pydicom logger should have 0 handlers (it doesn't add its own)")

# Check the pydicom logger
pydicom_logger = logging.getLogger('pydicom')
print(f"\nVerification:")
print(f"  pydicom logger handlers: {len(pydicom_logger.handlers)} (expected: 0)")
print(f"  pydicom logger level: {logging.getLevelName(pydicom_logger.level)}")

if len(pydicom_logger.handlers) == 0:
    print("\n✓ SUCCESS: pydicom does not add its own handler!")
    print("  Users now have full control over logging configuration.")
else:
    print(f"\n✗ FAILURE: pydicom added {len(pydicom_logger.handlers)} handlers")
    print("  This means users get duplicate log messages.")
EOF"}

Now, there's one more thing I noticed: the debug message "Reversing DICOM dictionary..." appears when pydicom is imported. This is happening at module level in datadict.py. While this is a separate issue from the one described in the PR (which was about duplicate messages), it might still be undesirable to have debug messages logged during import. However, changing that would require modifying datadict.py, which is outside the scope of this PR. The PR specifically addresses the issue of pydicom defining its own handler, formatter, and log level, which we've fixed.

Let me also check if there are any other places in the codebase where logging might be configured. Let me search for other logging configurations:

messages.content:
Good! The patch shows:
1. Removed the handler, formatter, and `logger.addHandler(handler)` lines
2. Added `debugging = False` initialization at module level
3. Removed the `debug(False)` call at the end (which was forcing the log level to WARNING)

This addresses all the issues mentioned in the PR:
- pydicom no longer defines its own handler
- pydicom no longer defines its own formatter  
- pydicom no longer sets the log level by default
- Client code now has full control over logging configuration

Let me run one final test to make sure everything still works:

function.arguments:
{"command": "cat > final_test.py << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env python
import logging

# Test 1: User configures logging before importing pydicom
print("Test 1: User configures logging before import")
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='USER: %(name)s - %(message)s')
import pydicom
logger = logging.getLogger('pydicom')
logger.info("Test message 1")
print(f"Handlers on pydicom logger: {len(logger.handlers)} (expected: 0)\n")

# Test 2: User configures logging after importing pydicom  
print("Test 2: User configures logging after import")
import logging as logging2
import pydicom as pydicom2
logging2.basicConfig(level=logging2.DEBUG, format='USER2: %(name)s - %(message)s')
logger2 = logging2.getLogger('pydicom')
logger2.debug("Test message 2")
print(f"Handlers on pydicom logger: {len(logger2.handlers)} (expected: 0)\n")

# Test 3: User doesn't configure logging at all
print("Test 3: No user logging configuration")
import logging as logging3
import pydicom as pydicom3
logger3 = logging3.getLogger('pydicom')
print(f"Handlers on pydicom logger: {len(logger3.handlers)} (expected: 0)")
print(f"Logger level: {logging3.getLevelName(logger3.level)} (expected: NOTSET)")
print("(No log messages will appear because no handlers are configured)\n")

print("All tests passed! pydicom no longer interferes with logging configuration.")
EOF"}
